name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q1 - VERIS Acronym

answer.text: Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing

feedback.text: Correct! VERIS is the Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing, developed by Verizon's Risk Team.

answer.text: Vulnerability Event Reporting and Intelligence Sharing

feedback.text: Incorrect. VERIS focuses on incident recording, not vulnerability reporting.
Incorrect. VERIS stands for Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing.

answer.text: Variable Event Response and Incident System

feedback.text: Incorrect. VERIS is Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing.

questiontext.text: Which of the following is NOT one of the four dimensions in the VERIS 4-A framework?

feedback.text: Correct! The 4-A framework consists of Actor, Action, Asset, and Attribute. Alert is not a dimension.
Incorrect. Actor (who caused the incident) is one of the four A dimensions in VERIS.
Incorrect. Action (what was done) is one of the four A dimensions in VERIS.
Incorrect. Asset (what was targeted) is one of the four A dimensions in VERIS.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q3 - Misconfiguration Action

questiontext.text: A developer accidentally misconfigures a cloud database to be publicly accessible, exposing 500,000 customer records. What is the correct VERIS action type?

feedback.text: Correct! Accidental misconfiguration is classified as Error in VERIS — specifically variety "Misconfiguration".
Incorrect. Hacking implies intentional technical exploitation. This was an unintentional configuration mistake.
Incorrect. Misuse requires intentional unauthorized use of legitimate access. This was accidental.
Incorrect. Physical actions involve tangible physical access to systems. This was a cloud configuration error.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q4 - data_disclosure Field

questiontext.text: In VERIS, what does the data_disclosure field inside the confidentiality attribute section represent?

answer.text: Whether an unauthorized party is confirmed to have accessed the data

feedback.text: Correct! data_disclosure indicates whether unauthorized access to the data was confirmed (Yes), not confirmed (No), or uncertain (Unknown).

answer.text: Whether the organization disclosed the breach to regulators

feedback.text: Incorrect. VERIS captures technical incident data, not regulatory reporting actions.

answer.text: The type of encryption used to protect the data

feedback.text: Incorrect. Encryption type is captured in the "state" field, not data_disclosure.

answer.text: Whether the data was encrypted at the time of the incident

feedback.text: Incorrect. Encryption state is captured in the "state" sub-field (e.g., "Stored unencrypted").

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q5 - Timeline Discovery

questiontext.text: Which VERIS timeline field captures how long it took the organization to detect the incident after it occurred?

feedback.text: Correct! timeline.discovery records how long from the incident occurrence to when it was discovered by the organization.
Incorrect. timeline.incident records when the incident occurred (year, month), not the time to detection.
Incorrect. timeline.exfiltration records how long from access to data leaving the organization.
Incorrect. timeline.compromise records how long from the attack start to successful initial compromise.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q6 - Multiple Action Types

questiontext.text: An organized crime group sends phishing emails to employees of a bank and uses the stolen credentials to log into the online banking system. How many VERIS action types should be recorded?

feedback.text: Correct! The phishing = social engineering, and using stolen credentials = hacking. Both action types must be recorded to capture the full attack chain.

answer.text: One — only the phishing action matters

feedback.text: Incorrect. Both stages of the attack chain must be recorded. The credential use (hacking) is a distinct action.

answer.text: One — only the credential use matters

feedback.text: Incorrect. The initial phishing (social engineering) is also an action and should be recorded.

answer.text: Three — social engineering, hacking, and malware

feedback.text: Incorrect. There is no malware in this scenario. Only two action types apply: social and hacking.

answer.text: A public, community-contributed dataset of VERIS-coded incidents

feedback.text: Correct! The VERIS Community Database (VCDB) is an open, publicly available collection of VERIS-coded security incidents.

answer.text: A commercial tool for security incident management

feedback.text: Incorrect. VCDB is a free, open dataset — not a commercial product.

answer.text: Verizon's internal database of breach cases used only for DBIR

feedback.text: Incorrect. The VCDB is publicly available on GitHub and accepts community contributions.

answer.text: A government threat intelligence sharing platform

feedback.text: Incorrect. VCDB is a community-run project, not a government platform.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q8 - Insider Data Theft

questiontext.text: An employee deliberately copies 10,000 customer records to a personal USB drive before resigning. Which VERIS actor type and action type best describe this incident?

feedback.text: Correct! The employee is an internal actor with legitimate access, and deliberately copying data for unauthorized purposes is Misuse (variety: "Privilege abuse" or "Data mishandling").
Incorrect. The employee is an internal actor, not external. The action is Misuse, not Hacking.
Incorrect. Error implies an unintentional action. Deliberately copying data is intentional — it is Misuse.
Incorrect. A Partner actor is from a third-party organization. An employee is an Internal actor.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q9 - Asset Type Prefix

questiontext.text: Which VERIS asset type prefix is used to categorize a database server?

feedback.text: Correct! S stands for Server in VERIS. A database server would be coded as "S - Database".
Incorrect. U stands for User Device (e.g., laptops, desktops), not servers.
Incorrect. N stands for Network (e.g., firewalls, routers), not servers.
Incorrect. M stands for Media (e.g., USB drives, backup tapes), not servers.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q10 - Malware Action

questiontext.text: In VERIS, which action category covers the installation of keyloggers or backdoors on a compromised system?

feedback.text: Correct! Keyloggers and backdoors are types of malicious software — they fall under the Malware action category in VERIS.
Incorrect. Social actions involve human manipulation (e.g., phishing). Installing software on a system is Malware.
Incorrect. Hacking covers technical exploitation to gain access. The software installed after access is classified as Malware.
Incorrect. Misuse involves unauthorized use of legitimate access, not malicious software installation.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q11 - Ransomware Attributes

questiontext.text: An organization suffers a ransomware attack that encrypts all files on 50 servers but no data is confirmed as exfiltrated. Which VERIS CIA attributes should be recorded?

feedback.text: Correct! Availability is impacted (files inaccessible) and Integrity is impacted (ransomware installed). Confidentiality should NOT be recorded without confirmed exfiltration — only record confirmed impacts.
Incorrect. There is no confirmed data disclosure. Confidentiality should not be recorded without evidence of unauthorized data access.
Incorrect. Adding Confidentiality without confirmed exfiltration would misrepresent the incident. VERIS records only confirmed impacts.
Incorrect. Integrity is also impacted because ransomware was installed on the systems (action: Malware / Install code).

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q12 - Shared Vocabulary Benefit

questiontext.text: Which of the following is a key benefit of using a shared vocabulary like VERIS for incident recording?

answer.text: It enables statistical comparison and trend analysis across organizations

feedback.text: Correct! Consistent terminology enables aggregation of data across organizations, enabling meaningful trend analysis and benchmarking — the foundation of the DBIR.

answer.text: It replaces the need for a SIEM in the SOC

feedback.text: Incorrect. VERIS is an incident recording standard, not a detection or monitoring tool.

answer.text: It automatically generates incident response playbooks

feedback.text: Incorrect. VERIS documents what happened; it does not generate response playbooks.

answer.text: It provides real-time threat intelligence feeds

feedback.text: Incorrect. VERIS is a retrospective recording standard, not a real-time threat intelligence platform.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q13 - DBIR Data Source

questiontext.text: Which publication uses VERIS-coded data as its primary data source for annual threat landscape statistics?

answer.text: The Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR)

feedback.text: Correct! The DBIR is published annually by Verizon and uses VERIS-coded incident data from Verizon and partner contributors.
Incorrect. The NIST CSF is a risk management framework, not an annual threat statistics report.
Incorrect. ATT&CK documents adversary techniques but does not use VERIS as its primary data source.
Incorrect. SANS Internet Stormcast is a daily threat briefing, not an annual VERIS-based statistical report.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q14 - Exploit Vulnerability

questiontext.text: An attacker exploits an unpatched Apache vulnerability to gain root access to a web server. What is the correct VERIS action type and variety?

feedback.text: Correct! Exploiting a software vulnerability to gain unauthorized access is Hacking with variety "Exploit vulnerability".
Incorrect. The action of exploiting the vulnerability is Hacking. Malware would be recorded separately only if malicious software was also installed.
Incorrect. Error is for unintentional actions. This was a deliberate attack using a known vulnerability.
Incorrect. Phishing is a social engineering technique. Exploiting a software vulnerability is Hacking.

name.text: VERIS Final Quiz Q15 - Nation-State Actor

questiontext.text: A nation-state group compromises a defense contractor's network for espionage purposes. Which VERIS actor type would be most appropriate?

feedback.text: Correct! Nation-state actors are classified as External in VERIS, with variety "Nation-state". Their motive is typically "Espionage".
Incorrect. An internal actor belongs to the victim organization. A nation-state group is an external threat actor.
Incorrect. Partner actors have a business relationship with the victim. A nation-state adversary has no such relationship.
Incorrect. While nation-states are external, their specific variety is "Nation-state", not "Organized crime". The distinction matters for motive and attribution.
